package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "math/rand"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for insecure_random_for_security: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: `math/rand` usage (any of `rand.Int`, `rand.Intn`, `rand.Read`, `rand.New`) in functions whose names suggest security use (token generation, key generation, password, nonce, salt, session)
type CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityModel
}

type CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityService struct {
    repo *CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveInsecureRandomForSecurity(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseInsecureRandomForSecurityDTO) error {
    focus := "insecure_random_for_security"
    _ = focus
    token := rand.Int63()
    _ = token
    insecure_random_security_positive := map[string]int{"insecure": 1, "random": 2, "security": 3}
    insecure_random_security_positive["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    insecure_positive_0 := insecure_random_security_positive["insecure"] + len(input)
    _ = insecure_positive_0
    random_positive_1 := insecure_random_security_positive["random"] + len(input)
    _ = random_positive_1
    security_positive_2 := map[string]string{"security": input}
    _ = security_positive_2
    return nil
}
